items.title: Warframe Tau Update: New Solar System, Enemies, and Warframes Coming in 2026
items.summary: In the Warframe universe, Tau is a topic that has come up a number of times throughout the game's lore, but, as announced by Digital Extremes at TennoCon 2026, players will finally be able to experience some of the Tau solar system in 2026 when the Warframe Tau update launches. The Tau update...

items.title: Destiny 2 dying is "existentially threatening" for game devs in the industry, says Warframe director
items.summary: Warframe creative director Rebecca Ford says that the sudden death of Bungie
items.content: 1 Report: Microsoft set to make even more of its games exclusive to Xbox

items.title: Warframe of Mind: Reacting to the big reveals of TennoCon 2026
items.content:
Hey, folks! What were you up to this weekend? I guess there was a Warframe thing? I don’t know, I wasn’t paying much attention. Have you been watching the World Cup? That’s been crazy this year.

All right, obviously that’s not real; no one who reads my work would believe that I am following the World Cup with anything approaching enthusiasm or that I wasn’t watching TennoCon with rapt attention. I most certainly was watching the reveals live in-game at the relay, and I’ve been itching to chat about all of this to the point that we moved this column to Monday just for the event. And boy-howdy, did we ever get a pile of revelations at the event itself.

As sometimes happens, I’m especially curious about the things that we also haven’t heard about just yet. We know a bit of the stuff that’s coming through the rest of the year ahead of Tau, but the reveal was very much centered around Tau. While we’ve heard a very little bit of the Iceblade of Narin, I’d be lying if I said that didn’t have more than a little fascination for me in and of itself.

Obviously, the key point of interest for me is the actual content contained therein. It’s been a long time since we’ve gotten a new Warframe that isn’t also the center of a new piece of content, so I imagine Narin will be similar, and I definitely feel like there’s an Ostron vibe there. Considering the last Ostron-focused Warframe we got, Koumei, there’s obvious ground to cover here with a yuki-onna sort of frame, giving us the frost theme and potentially a new place to take things.

The Banshee rework also has me interested, since contrary to the assertions on the stage Banshee really does lack in a lot of fields. It’s not that she’s a terrible frame who doesn’t work at all (she’s not Limbo or Loki) but her passive isn’t useful at all and her abilities are generally not very worthwhile; I find myself wondering exactly what Pablo in particular thinks just needs to be drawn out. That being said, the track record for reworks is solid, so I’m definitely going to have a Banshee Prime ready to go ahead of the now-very-announced Fall update.

But there’s only so much to be said about that update when the centerpiece is Tau. And Tau is… well, at this point, tantalizing. What we’ve seen thus far on Fornax is so little of what we’re ultimately going to get, but the tease of the city alone shows a completely alien environment, one that is as shaped by the artificial life that dwells there that the line between structure and being is blurred to an outside observer.

The presence of Brysko, much like Sirius & Orion, throws a lovely wrench into the narratives we have long absorbed about the frames themselves, as well. For most of the game’s history, Warframes have been ancient things, but I find it impossible to contextualize Brysko as anything but new . Being a Chimeraframe means something we may not yet know, but the reality is that he is not a usual frame as we understand them beyond the pure mechanical side of things.

What do I mean by that? Brysko is not the name of a character who happens to use this frame. He is not Roathe, who led to the creation of Uriel; he is not Arthur Nightingale occupying a similar place to Excalibur. Brysko is a Warframe. Granted, the fact that he is an artificial consciousness within one also leads to some variants, but it also means that it’s possible we’re going to get a further reworking of what Transference and building new frames actually entails in a way that hasn’t happened for a whole.

On top of that, of course, are further narrative questions. What happened between the two points that connected Adis to the Hunra? How far does the Hunra’s reach extend? What does the larger state of Tau look like, aside from the fact that Entrati clearly expects the clash between the Tenno and the Hunra that is surely arriving? There are so many question marks that it feels impossible to even start parsing them, even aside from the fact that we’ve yet to see most of the quest.

Mechanically, it looks like Brysko is going to be a pretty straightforward frame, which makes a certain amount of sense – after Sirius & Orion and Follie ratcheted complexity up a bit, it makes sense to pull things back down a little. I’m also curious if the Hallvu we got is a preview of one of the weapons we’ll have access to from this content, which would both make sense and suggests an interesting set of new weapon designs after what had, otherwise, been a fairly firm set of rules.

If there’s a concern that I have, it’s that right now some of the lack of information we have reminds me in a not-wholly-positive fashion of the Old Peace. See, The Old Peace had two main arms of content – the Perita Rebellion and the Descendia – and while the two overlapped somewhat, they never really connected all that firmly. Furthermore, I always felt as if that lack of connective tissue was tangible. The lack of any kind of syndicate, the very loose structure of Tauron Strikes as a reward, the general grind just did not quite work as well as it could have.

Obviously, there isn’t a similar tease going on here, but we know that Fornax is just the beginning of Tau. I can completely believe that our initial missions are going to be centered around Fornax and perhaps one other location, dealing with Hunra enemies, and so forth. But a lot of the things I hoped to see from the Old Peace – more of the old Grineer, for example – didn’t come to pass, and part of the reason feels like that disconnect.

That’s not to say I’m not excited about Tau, just that I want to see more of our gameplay loop and I hope it’s going to be a bit more engaging as a whole. Things for the future, I suppose.

With that mild worry out of the way, though, it definitely is a huge event for the game and a huge milestone. Up until now, you could argue that Warframe is the story of a humanity that tried to leave the cradle of our origins and failed. But with this update, that is no longer true. It’s been a very long time, but humanity is once more stepping out into the wider universe.

items.title: “View From The Top” Official Song Release
items.summary: TennoLive 2026 swept us away beyond the boundaries of the Origin System to far-flung Tau and the smoky gambling dens of Fornax. Doomy, gloomy, yet undeniably stylish, our Gameplay Demo's seductive allure was all the more potent thanks to th
items.content:
TennoLive 2026 swept us away beyond the boundaries of the Origin System to far-flung Tau and the smoky gambling dens of Fornax. Doomy, gloomy, yet undeniably stylish, our Gameplay Demo's seductive allure was all the more potent thanks to the sultry sounds of a particular musical number sung by Sentient crooner Larynde. We know how difficult it can be to keep such a beautifully brooding ballad out of one's head, so we've made it available to stream anytime you'd like through your platform of choice. If you haven't heard it yet (or just want an immediate re-listen), the visualizer for "View From The Top" is also now available on the YouTube Channel.

Conceived, performed and produced in-house by Warframe's unstoppable sound team, this might just be our most cinematic soundscape yet. Let us know what you think on our social channels, and please enjoy!

LISTEN NOW:

  • Spotify
  • Apple Music
  • Bandcamp
  • YouTube Music
  • Amazon
  • Tidal

CREDITS

Composer: Matthew Chalmers Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers Vocals: Jill Harris Lyrics by: Matthew Chalmers, Francesca Hauser

LYRICS

Keeping steady, I'm pacing just passing on by Heavy handed, I'm playing up on a live wire You could be anyone Another face in the view So what would you do with my love If I could give it to you? In another dimension I could be what you need Before you go and chase it Share the view from the top with me We start up in full bloom And waste away the afternoon Holding onto the thrill of it Frozen in time (in tiiiiiiiime) You could be anyone Another face in the scene So you might the be the winning kind But you’re not winning with me In another dimension I could be what you need Before you go and chase it Share the view from the top with me

items.title: Destiny 2’s Demise Is ‘Existentially Threatening,’ Says ‘Warframe’ Creative Director
items.summary: Another game in the live-service shooter genre, Warframe, has been running in parallel with Destiny for ages, maintaining perhaps greater consistency over time,
items.content:
Gaming Destiny 2’s Demise Is ‘Existentially Threatening,’ Says ‘Warframe’ Creative Director By Paul Tassi ,

Forbes contributors publish independent expert analyses and insights. News and opinion about video games, television, movies and the internet. Follow Author Jul 13, 2026, 11:11am EDT Jul 13, 2026, 04:46pm EDT --:-- / --:-- This voice experience is generated by AI. Learn more . This voice experience is generated by AI. Learn more . Warframe/Marathon Digital Extremes, Bungie Well, this would happen in any normal season of Destiny 2, but the game is starting its downward slope in terms of players departing. It is not an abrupt drop-off, which is great to see, and it’s still performing well a month after its final update, Monument of Triumph. But there’s the rub, the final update. Nothing else is coming here for the formerly live game.

Another game in the live-service shooter genre, Warframe , has been running in parallel with Destiny for ages, maintaining perhaps greater consistency over time, and still surviving, even thriving to this day. But rather than celebrate a “win” over the departed Destiny , it’s making its higher-ups sad and nervous. Some new comments come from Warframe Creative Director Rebb Ford in an interview with GamesRadar during the recent TennoCon:

"It's horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark.”

"Those are the types of stories and experiences that, when you're in a position where you have your own game, your own IP, and you work as hard as you do on it... That's not the first time it's happened, and it'll happen again, where the business aspect of the video game economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

Destiny 2 Bungie In the case of Destiny , there were years-long plans for the second game, and if it was up to the devs at Bungie, most would eagerly have hopped on board the production of a Destiny 3. But cost, cost, cost killed the game, partly due to poor management decisions that exacerbated those costs, and partly due to the baseline of a game that requires such an enormous investment to maintain endless live content. As I’ve previously reported, there have only been a few times in its history that Destiny 2 has been profitable.

Right until the end, you could feel the passion of the Destiny devs with an exceptional final update that put the game in its best state in years…right before there was no more new content to play. The player surge was higher than both of the last two expansions despite almost nothing new to play, and again, it maintains good numbers after a month. But those will continue falling, which cannot be helped.

Ford may be right to be nervous about the state of the industry and working on a very similar game. Warframe’s Digital Extremes does not have to answer to Sony, however, and costs are not at the same level as Destiny . But they’ve figured out a sustainable groove, and there’s no sign of the game ending any time remotely soon.

Follow me on Twitter , YouTube , and Instagram .

Pick up my sci-fi novels the Herokiller series and The Earthborn Trilogy .

items.title: TennoCon 2026: Every Big Warframe And Soulframe Announcement
Warframe creative director says Destiny 2's end is tragic, because even with hard work 'the business side of this industry always gets the last remark'
items.content:
"The idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Digital Extremes / Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Destiny 2 isn't going anywhere, but it also isn't… well, going anywhere. The game's servers will still be running for the foreseeable future, but Bungie has since ceased updates to its flagship shooter to move onto other projects, tragically laying off much of its staff in the aftermath of that decision.

It's a sorry state of affairs, and one that Warframe developers have lamented before , despite being in competition with the game from a ruthless, capitalistic sort of perspective. In actuality, there's a lot more camaraderie between developers, with live ops lead Megan Everett calling it "heartbreaking" back in June.

That sentiment has been repeated again by creative director Rebecca Ford in an interview with our friends over at GamesRadar+ during TennoCon: "It's horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark."

Ford says that even though the Destiny 2 team had their own successful IP and worked hard to keep it afloat, that kind of commitment just doesn't protect you from anything in the current industry:

"That's not the first time it's happened, and it'll happen again, where the business aspect of the videogame economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren't in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day."

'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable' Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors' 'Don't blame yourselves' for Destiny 2's end, community lead tells players: 'No words can accurately depict how lucky we are to have shared these worlds with you' It's something that's been grimly echoed throughout the industry, both during Microsoft's shock studio closures in 2024, which saw Hi-Fi Rush developer Tango Gameworks unceremoniously closed, and in the bloodbath years that've ensued from all corners of the videogame industry .

A greater shame, too, because according to those of us on the PC Gamer team who've stuck with Destiny 2 for years, it's been left in one of its best states —though as we've seen with other games, you can be genuinely turning a corner and still get sucker-punched .

items.title: TennoCon 2026: Everything Announced at the Warframe and Soulframe Fan Event
items.content: TennoCon 2026 has finally wrapped, and developer Digital Extremes has revealed new content for both Warframe and Soulframe – it's two massive online action RPGs. Throughout the TennoCon live show, we got to see the developers showcase long-anticipated updates that will not only take players to new locations but also introduce new antagonists for the larger stories ahead in both live-service games. More like this Soulframe: Preludes - Official Gameplay Trailer | IGN Summer of Gaming 2026 In addition to the long-form reveal of Warframe's Tau expansion, which introduces a new galaxy to explore that's filled with new threats, we saw Soulframe showcase a new fable questline focusing on the reveal of a new weapon and darkside powers to adopt, along with the anticipated arrival of new player mounts and a new fishing mini-game.

items.title: Warframe Dev Reacts To The Sudden Death Of Rival Destiny 2
items.summary: Business realities remain an existential threat to live service art
items.content:
Warframe launched a year before Destiny 2 and kicked off what would become one of the most vibrant and consistent MMO shooters around, especially on console. And despite spending many years in the shadow of Bungie’s live service blockbuster, Digital Extremes’ sci-fi odyssey now continues to thrive even as Destiny 2 comes to a sudden and anti-climactic end . Creative director Rebecca Ford was asked about the downfall of Warframe ‘s longtime rival at this weekend’s TennoCon 2026.

“It’s horrible news, because it shows that even if you care so much, the business side of this industry always gets the last remark,” Ford told GamesRadar . “Those are the types of stories and experiences that, when you’re in a position where you have your own game, your own IP, and you work as hard as you do on it…”

She continued, “That’s not the first time it’s happened, and it’ll happen again, where the business aspect of the video game economy makes the decision for you, and it is existentially threatening at every level, because the idea that we aren’t in charge of our own goodbye is something I wake up thinking about every single day.”

The unexpected sunsetting of Destiny 2 this summer shows what that nightmare scenario can look like. Work on a planned expansion instead shifts to a final update that leaves untold threads in the game dangling in the wind and the game itself in a state that’s far from ideal for a multiplayer shooter that will now be locked in amber for as long as its servers remain online. While it’s impossible to know what the game’s fate may have been if Bungie had remained independent instead of selling to Sony, it’s hard to imagine it wrapping up like this.

“I’m speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny , and its players and that story,” Warframe live ops lead Megan Everett told Eurogamer last month. “I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being ‘direct competitors’ and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors, and [ Destiny ‘s developers] have done such an amazing job at trying to grow that story regardless of whatever situation they were in.”

Digital Extremes is not completely independent either. Chinese games holding company Leyou owns a majority stake and was previously purchased by Tencent. The Canadian studio has also faced its own setbacks. It set out to publish Airship Syndicate’s MMO Wayfinder in 2023 before eventually having to cut it loose later that same year . Even as Digital Extremes prepares to launch a fantasy MMO companion to Warframe , its original sci-fi shooter continues to be as relevant as ever.

Don’t miss the latest reviews, news and tips. Sign up for our free newsletter.

items.title: TennoCon 2026: Everything you need to know about Warframe’s reveals from TennoLive
items.content:
Through the entirety of Warframe’s history, Tau has been the star in the distance. We’ve longed for Tau. We’ve been told of Tau. We’ve looked forward to Tau. And we were promised last year that we are, in fact, going to Tau. Now, with all of that preamble, out of the way, we are… going to Tau. But not until the end of the year, and there’s quite a bit to commend what we’ve seen other than Tau along the way.

Make no mistake, our journey to Tau was the biggest reveal from TennoLive this year, but there’s more to be seen in terms of reveals. We got a chance to see content for the next track of themed rewards for playing the game, a tease of the next major update for the game in the fall, and then a full shot of what we can look forward to when we arrive in Tau at the end of the year. And if you were hoping Tau would be a promised land free of strife… well, that’s very clearly not the case.

It’s been quite a while since Warframe had a full, proper event for Nightwave; we’ve been enjoying Nora’s mixes for a while now. So it’s perhaps ironic that the next such update will not actually feature Nora taking center stage, but everyone’s favorite hyperspeed protoframe, Amir. If you’ve done your Hex conversations, you know that Amir was hype to run a Fables & Frontiers game with the entire Hex, and with this event the game is finally on.

The game will be set up inside the mall in Hollvania, complete with a group chat for the game in the KIM system (there has to be a group chat, after all). While the exact rewards for this particular track are not fully revealed yet, players can look forward to glyphs of the Hex in their tabletop roleplaying personae (albeit not as new Protoframe skins) and a new On-Lyne track, “Running Late,” to go along with it. If you’ve longed to get a bit more traditional fantasy in your space ninja game, here you go. Amir’s Shockwave will be arriving in August, so you won’t have to wait long to roll for the initiative.

The Unannounced Fall Update is a staple of the Warframe update cycle, but this time around the update is announced! We know what it’s called! It’s Iceblade of Narin, and it’s bringing us back to what appear to be regions around Cetus and the Ostrons once more. Needless to say, this update will feature another new Warframe, Narin, and this one will be ice-themed (hence the name) since up until now Frost has been the only ice-themed Warframe. Might she have some kind of blade that is ice-related? Odds seem high.

The update is going to include more besides that, however; players can look forward to not just a new Banshee deluxe skin, but also a Banshee rework. Some people (this author) have previously said that Banshee isn’t a particularly good frame, but that just leaves her ripe for a rework, and with the darkly ominous deluxe skin she’s getting, she’ll be well-poised to take full advantage of it.

In addition, players can look forward to a new Qorvex deluxe skin giving him a rocky gargoyle-based appearance. And if you’re wondering “shouldn’t there be a new Prime in there,” you’re correct; Citrine is the next frame to get the Prime treatment, with her upgraded form available as part of the Iceblade of Narin update. Because diamonds are ice, too. (They’re not, I know that, but just go along with it for the moment.) More details will be revealed about this particular update in September; until then, you’ll just need to stay frosty.

Of course, the centerpiece is Tau itself. And Tau shows us what has become of the Sentients in the wake of the Old War… which isn’t anything good. Tau’s space is now what looks like a noir-esque nightmare, with a black rain falling across the surface of a planet that has a built-up sentient slum. In the presentation, this is how we’re introduced to our new narrative companion and upcoming Warframe Brysko, a hard-bitten cigar-chomping private eye that looks ready and able to go trawling through the mean city streets, with an internal monologue provided by Matt Mercer himself.

Brysko is apparently not a protoframe, but a chimeraframe. What’s a chimeraframe? It appears to be a marriage of Warframe and Sentient into one frame, beyond even what Caliban offers. And in the presentation we saw, Brysko is stuck doing work for local don known as The Hunra, sounding like nothing so much as a crime boss equivalent of Hunhow. We know that Brysko is aided by Albrecht Entrati, and he’s trying to make sure that things aren’t going wrong when the Tenno arrive… but he’s one man (well, frame) in a bad, bad town by the name of Fornax.

Our preview of Brysko includes a quick glance at his abilities including throwing playing cards and his monster of revolver for a sidearm that serves as his personal Exalted Weapon, as well as other Sentient weapons that seem to have evolved even beyond the shape-changing options we’ve seen in the past. Sentient technology has continued to evolve in the interim, and it has gotten weird .

The actual substance of the mission we see Brysko undertaking leads him to take down a pair of twins who can link up to fight him and also work separately, showing just how far the technology has come (and giving a preview of what is sure to be a recurring boss fight in the new content). We then see him going to meet with the Hunra directly, along with our Sentient singer having a brief (albeit one-sided) conversation with Brysko. And the implication is given to us that the Hunra is connected to Adis, our Sentient friend from the Old Peace.

Players can look forward to Tau as a whole new star chart, expanding on what’s already available in the game. While the developers are vowing not to forget about the Origin System, we’ll start off exploring Fornax while taking on the Fornax Drowners as a new enemy faction. They’ve evolved very differently to the Sentients we’re accustomed to dealing with in the Origin System, to be clear.

And when you need a break from fighting off your enemies, why not relax with an invigorating game of cards? Portau is a new card minigame being added with Tau, a chance for players to explore an alternate version of poker with its own suits and own rules of play. Risk it all or play it safe in the gambling dens of Fornax.

Of course, you’ve got your new music to go along with it; the sultry tones of “View from the Top” will be available on streaming music services in the near future, so stay tuned if you can’t get enough of the Sentient torch song.

items.title: Warframe: Tau - Official Gameplay Demo | TennoCon 2026
items.summary: Take a look at a gameplay demo for Warframe: Tau, Warframe's next major narrative chapter. Reach beyond the bounds of the Origin System and venture into a new Star Chart, the Tau System. Once a paradise built by the Sentients, Tau has been lost to war and ruin. Nowhere is this felt more keenly than on the black rain-soaked streets of Fornax, a colossal Sentient ring city. Here, the devastating downpour and its maddening influence run in rivulets from the heights of the Hunra's gambling dens, through the Docks, and into the most desperate Slums — where the residents have turned to the mind-altering Bloom in search of escape. There, Albrecht Entrati awaits the arrival of the Tenno after conscripting the inner monologuing and noir-inspired Warframe, Brysko, to infiltrate the Hunra's criminal underworld — a final gamble to avert Fornax falling to those with nothing to lose. Get ready to risk it all.

items.title: TennoCon 2026
items.summary: That's a wrap on TennoCon 2026! Thank you to everyone for celebrating the future of Warframe with us, whether you joined in-person or watched this year's incredible reveals from home. We couldn't do it without you. If you missed them live (
items.content:
That's a wrap on TennoCon 2026! Thank you to everyone for celebrating the future of Warframe with us, whether you joined in-person or watched this year's incredible reveals from home. We couldn't do it without you. If you missed them live (or simply want to re-watch), you can find the and full TennoLive VODs on our YouTube channel! For a closer, bonus look at the Art & Animation of Warframe, click here.

AWAKEN TO A NEW SYSTEM

Warframe: Tau | Official Trailer

The path to Tau will soon open, taking you beyond the boundaries of the Origin System for the very first time. Get ready to risk it all in a brand new region of space orbiting a binary star and teeming with new experiences.

Get ready to risk it all. Warframe: Tau arrives later this year.

TennoCon 2026 Gameplay Demo

During your first foray into the lost paradise that is the Tau System, you'll sneak into the heart of a Sentient city ruined by criminality and corruption. But this is only the beginning, and marks a new journey for Warframe with much more of Tau to come, including another as-yet-to-be-revealed location launching with the full update later in 2026. Stay tuned for more details soon.

TennoLive 2026 Relay

Those who entered this year's in-game TennoLive Relay bore witness to a wild new addition to the Tenno fleet: their very own Vessel. You can still jump in and experience it for yourself until the Relay closes on July 13 at 10 a.m. ET. Think you missed out? Think again! Starting July 13, any Tenno who has completed Vor's Prize will be able to see and personalize their Vessel from ANY Dry Dock on the Star Chart. Keep watch for Arturos and Zioa, who will answer their calling later this year…

Enter a Sentient Metropolis

Your journey in this new Star Chart begins in the Bloom-soaked decay of Fornax, a lawless ring city densely populated with all manner of degenerates and drenched in unending waves of madness-inducing black rain. Between the addicts in its slums and the thugs working its docks, reaching the casino at the heart of this urban hellscape requires quick wits and an even quicker trigger finger.

Scour the Streets with Brysko

A noir-inspired Warframe working with Albrecht Entrati to infiltrate the Sentient criminal underworld, Brysko chases down leads with a relentlessness matched only by his cynicism. Although silent to the world, his inner monologue belies an honorable ethos… at least until the occasion calls for his Corecracker or Rain & Shine — an Exalted Pistol and a pair of brutal Fist weapons, respectively.

Tangle with the Fornax Drowners

The enforcers, hired hands and knucklebusters standing in your way comprise a new faction of Sentient enemies who have evolved far and away from the familiar foes of the Origin System. Though you're more than equipped to take them out, don't get too cocky, especially when the black rain begins to fall…

Defy the Hunra

Control over the many rackets in Fornax rests with the Hunra, a brutal kingpin at the center of the Sentient underbelly who enthusiastically dispenses violence, fear and Bloom to maintain his seat at the head of the table. To reach him, you'll need to overcome his legions of loyalists, any of whom would happily dispatch you to curry favor with the Boss, who seems as untouchable as he is implacable.

Try your hand at Portau

Fortune rarely smiles in Fornax, but when it does, the gamblers hunched at the casino's Portau tables earn the closest thing here to an honest living. Warframe's first original card game is built on unique suits of Blooms, Cores, Moons and Suns, and offers some welcome excitement amidst the hustle and heartbreak of Tau. Why not test your luck and play a few hands for yourself?

New Music: "View From The Top"

Doomy, gloomy, yet undeniably stylish, our Gameplay Demo's seductive allure was all the more potent thanks to the sultry sounds of a particular musical number sung by Sentient crooner Larynde. If you haven't heard it yet (or just want an immediate re-listen), the visualizer for "View From The Top" is also now available on the Official Warframe YouTube Channel.

COMING THIS FALL

Iceblade of Narin

Our Fall 2026 update has been named! Before you venture beyond the Origin System to Tau, we will first introduce the 66th Warframe, two new Deluxe Collections, a highly-anticipated Rework, the next Prime Access release and more. Join us for our next Devstream on September 4 to learn more.

Banshee Deluxe and Rework

Darkness comes for the scream queen with her first Deluxe Collection since 2016: a suite of Customizations evoking nightmares and the nocturnal, designed by community artist Mischka. Banshee mains can also look forward to an upcoming Rework that will improve her sound-based Abilities and overall playability.

Qorvex Deluxe

We're working hard to spruce up Albrecht Entrati's original designs for his indomitable, concrete-based Warframe, Qorvex. With a new Skin and Customizations inspired by gargoyles and gothic architecture, this radioactive behemoth has never looked better.

Citrine Prime Access

The luminously lustrous Citrine will receive a Prime polishing when she enters Prime Access later this year, crystalizing her greatness with pristine, golden glam. She'll release alongside upgraded Prime Weapons, Accessories and more, which we'll reveal in greater detail soon, so stay tuned.

SOULFRAME

Warsongs: A Soulframe Fable

A splinter in the heart, sings in the dream.

Meet Bayor, an Envoy like yourself but sworn to Synod and the Ode. Venture back to memories of your childhood with Doyen. Ride into the fray on wolf-back as you confront the threat in the present. Embrace the corruption of the Vadagar as a new path to progression in Soulframe.

Full Soulframe Recap

Read the complete event recap for more details on the unveiling of a new Pact, the upcoming addition of fishing, long-awaited mounts and the Mendicant Reinbreaker which awaits your challenge in-game right now. Or hear the details from the developers themselves with the TennoCon 2026 Soulframe Devstream, available here.

EVENTS

Amir's Shockwave

Get ready for a shakeup like you've never heard before! This August, Amir of the Hex will be cutting through the airwaves (and across space and time) to bring you a double-dose of 1999-themed content. Not only will he be hosting his own remixed edition of Nightwave replete with never-before-seen Rewards, but he'll also be joining his Protoframe teammates for a unique KIM narrative adventure, Fables & Frontiers: Running Late.

TennoVIP 2026 Tour

We're set to connect with more Tenno than ever thanks to a host of new TennoVIP events around the world. With Warframe developers set to visit South Korea, China, London and Glasgow, this could be your chance to meet up for the ultimate IRL experience. Ticket registration for future stops will go live at a later date, so keep an eye on our official social channels.

Dog Days 2026

What's summer without a trip to the beach? Warframe's hottest seasonal event returns on August 4 with sizzling Rewards including four new Glyphs designed by community artist SpaceWaifu.

Warframe x Fortnite

Warframe's original swordsman brings his mastery of the blade to the world of Fortnite! For the first time, Tenno can squad up, drop into the Island, and outrun the storm as the legendary Warframe with a brand-new Excalibur Outfit.

PACKS & MERCH

Mesa Heirloom Collection

Our latest hallowed Heirloom Collection is now available! The legendary gunslinger, Mesa, has new Customizations to keep your spurs jingling, including her signature brimmed Signa and travel-worn Duster attachments featuring additional toggles so you can always adjust her look to your liking. https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735410/Warframe_Mesa_Heirloom_Collection/

TennoCon 2026 Merch

Warframe's biggest day of the year may be over, but you can still keep the party going with the TennoCon 2026 Merch Pack. Show the world you were there when Warframe moved beyond the Origin System to Tau with this year's exclusive IRL Customizations.

TennoCon 2026 Giveaways

There's still time to enter our celebratory giveaways and secure your chance to win some truly stellar prizes — including gaming gear from HyperX, the Stalker jersey from Champion, Platinum from yours truly, and a powerful, customized PC from OMEN. Good luck, Tenno! Enter Now!

Community

Cosplay Contest

Our annual Cosplay Contest returned to the Canada Life Place, with a fresh roster of designs from around the Origin System. Contenders were judged on performance and craftsmanship by special guest judges, to the resounding cheers of their fellow Tenno.

Art Showcase

The Origin System’s finest artists delivered once again! Contest finalists saw their work displayed for all to behold at TennoCon — a testament to the passion and creativity of Tenno from all over the world.

Dojo Showcase

Presenting your winners of the #TennoCon 2026 Dojo Showcase! Participating decorators were invited to submit their best in-game builds from over the past two years. With more than 75 submissions from three different Warframe builds, narrowing down the winners was no easy feat.

SPECIAL THANKS

Sistema Toronto

Sistema Toronto is a free after-school music and social development program for children living in underserved communities with the end goal of transformative social action. Your donations allow Sistema Toronto students to play real instruments, receive instruction with professional musicians, eat a healthy snack every day, and benefit from a community of support.

London Cares

London Cares Homeless Response Services delivers frontline support to chronic homelessness through street outreach, supportive housing, and housing stability services. By integrating housing with health, income, and community supports, they help people achieve lasting stability while strengthening the systems that serve them

Hoku Props

Award winning propmakers Hoku Props returned to TennoCon with another iconic cosplay: Father Lyon Allard, the Harrow Protoframe! Meticulously crafted with an astounding attention to detail, this was one of their most faithful designs yet — an impressive tribute to an iconic character from last year's The Old Peace (and his many devoted fans).

arborealkey

France-based costume and prop creator arborealkey crafted a most captivating cosplay for Sister Marie Leroux, and her ethereal presence was one of the highlights of the showfloor here in London, Ontario.

Blink

Blink certainly stepped up to the challenge with their latest project: Old War veteran and infamous heartthrob, Roathe. With a loving eye and the incredible attention to detail they're known for, they brought this fearsome figure to life as faithfully as we could have fathomed.

Plexi Cosplay

Plexi Cosplay brought to life the Brewer of Hues, Verminia! Delighting Soulframe fans on the convention floor with astute aura assessments, her presence was as lively as her designs are delightful.

items.title: Warframe: Tau Gameplay Demo
items.summary: This year's TennoLive mainstage presentation took us beyond the limits of the Origin System to Tau, a place that until now has only existed in our collective dreams (and of course, memories via The Old Peace). Community Director Megan Evere
items.content: This year's TennoLive mainstage presentation took us beyond the limits of the Origin System to Tau, a place that until now has only existed in our collective dreams (and of course, memories via The Old Peace). Community Director Megan Everett guided Tenno through their first foray into Warframe's first ENTIRELY NEW star system, which will include fresh worlds to explore as ongoing cinematic narrative flourishes in a massive new region beyond anything you've ever known. Coming to all platforms later this year, our next major update will begin to answer the question: "What has become of Tau?" While the future will ultimately offer a wider look at this vast binary star system, the path to get there begins in Fornax — a sprawling Sentient metropolis consisting of Slums, Docks, and seedy gambling dens. The extent of Fornax's corruption is apparent in both the addicts and criminals who populate it, as well as the unending black rain that pours down on them during frequent storms… with horrifying results. Albrecht Entrati is already here, having enlisted the help of a new, noir-inspired Warframe named Brysko to help him infiltrate the Sentient underworld and reach the Hunra. This fearsome kingpin maintains control of Fornax's many rackets through violence, intimidation and fear, and it will be up to you to reach him in time to avert the breakout of a cataclysmic new conflict. With the return of old friends, old foes and old wounds gone-but-not-forgotten, Tau will confront you with horrifying consequences from your past, and an incredibly bright new canvas for the evolving future of Warframe. Learn more by visiting our official webpage for Warframe: Tau here and be sure to watch our upcoming Devstreams at twitch.tv/warframe for more details on the journey that lies ahead.

items.title: Warframe is showing love to tabletop games with this ‘legally distinct’ RPG
items.summary: Amir finally gets to play Fables & Frontiers this August
items.content:
By Simone de Rochefort Published Jul 11, 2026, 6:01 PM EDT Gaming Amir finally gets to play Fables & Frontiers this August

All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 You can play Warframe’s fantasy successor, Soulframe, for free right now Warframe is showing love to tabletop games with this ‘legally distinct’ RPG Image: Digital Extremes Warframe Sign in to your Polygon.com account Dungeons & Dragons is in the air at Digital Extremes. The developer is introducing a new minigame to Warframe : a “legally distinct” roleplaying game called Fables & Frontiers .

Longtime players will recognize the name. It’s the fantasy tabletop game that Amir, one of the 1999 expansion protoframes, desperately wants to play with the rest of the Hex. Well, it’s finally time for Amir’s dream of playing a game with his friends to come true.

“This particular mini adventure is staying forever as a permanent expansion to the Hex,” Warframe creative director Rebecca Ford told Polygon in a pre-TennoCon 2026 interview. “What we found was as we move away from 1999 , year over year, it's just too important to leave.”

Warframe: 1999 expanded the human cast of the game for the first time, introducing protoframes that hold the line for humanity against an alien infestation. Also, you can kiss them . Now the game’s narrative is pushing toward a new star system with the upcoming Tau expansion, but the protoframes are beloved characters whose stories still matter to the community.

Image: Digital Extremes “People for a long time have been like, ‘When is Amir ever gonna play [...] Fables & Frontiers ’?” said community director Megan Everett. “And so I think this is a nice little community love letter.”

A dedicated space in the Höllvania Mall will be redecorated as Amir’s F&F campaign room, complete with maps, game pieces, and the ability to summon Amir in a flash. The minigame itself is a text-based adventure that happens in the K.I.M. messaging system, which players already use to build relationships with the Hex.

Fables & Frontiers became a real, playable minigame within Warframe because tabletop roleplaying is not a fad for the development team at Digital Extremes.

“It's been a lifelong hobby for a lot of us,” said Ford. “We used to play Pathfinder , Dungeons & Dragons , and then it became a community team thing.”

Fables & Frontiers was included as a reference in the 1999 expansion as a gesture of “verisimilitude,” said Ford. “And then the campaign idea came together when we said we wanna do a Nightwave that is specifically focused on 1999, give it some flavor,” she said.

Image: Digital Extremes Fittingly then, the free Nightwave battle pass system will be taken over by Amir this August, in an event called Amir’s Shockwave. Shockwave will feature themed daily and weekly quests with new rewards — one of which is the new On-Lyne song, “Running Late.” It’s against this backdrop that players will be able to enjoy Fables & Frontiers .

The D&D flavor doesn’t end there, though, because acclaimed DM and voice actor Matthew Mercer is joining the Warframe cast with the Tau expansion, as the detective-themed Warframe Brysko.

items.title: Warframe's Next Expansion Is the Epic Payoff to 13 Years of Storytelling
items.summary: Warframe Tau might be Digital Extremes' version of Bungie's Destiny 2 expansion The Final Shape.
items.content:
Over the past few years, Warframe players have spent much of their time in-game exploring a broken timestream, jumping from one era to the next in their fight against an eldritch space god .

But at its TennoCon 2026 event, developer Digital Extremes revealed a bold new direction for the game: Instead of exploring time, players will soon enter an entirely new part of outer space. The Tau solar system teased in The Old Peace update will soon be accessible during regular gameplay, with multiple new celestial bodies for players to explore.

Live-service games that thrive for years get the rare chance to morph and evolve into something new. When Warframe launched in 2013, it was a simple third-person looter-shooter about deadly robotic ninjas. Now it features raid bosses, an endless roguelike mode, a semi-open-world fishing simulator and even some visual novel elements .

In an industry that seems increasingly reluctant to take risks on new ideas, Digital Extremes continues to reinvent its sci-fi brainchild. Each new update includes creative leaps leading to "out-there" ideas like a living guitar or a spider-themed robot , ensuring there's no other game quite like Warframe.

Even so, all those zany ideas existed within a single solar system -- disparate story threads connected by a common location. The boundaries of the world have always been well-defined by the in-game star chart. In the next expansion, that's changing for the first time ever.

Leaving behind the Origin System will introduce players to worlds that are more alien than they've ever seen before.

Warframe players have been hacking, slashing and bullet-jumping across the Origin System -- a starchart that mirrors our real-world solar system with some sci-fi alterations -- for over 13 years. Soon it'll be time to explore a new frontier: Tau.

It's difficult to express how massive this change feels without pointing toward the other sci-fi looter-shooter cultural juggernaut, Bungie's Destiny and Destiny 2.

The Destiny series -- first released in 2014, just one year after Warframe -- also took place largely within the boundaries of our very own solar system. For years, players battled the forces of darkness across the surface of Earth, Mars and other familiar planets.

But when Destiny 2's decade-long narrative reached its climax in The Final Shape expansion , players plunged into the heart of the living planet-like Traveler in order to battle the villainous Witness. When the stakes were highest, players were transported to a completely alien locale within the deity-like sphere they'd followed for Destiny's entire plot, a place where everyone was on equal footing and many long-standing questions were finally answered.

Warframe Tau looks like it'll provide answers to some longstanding questions -- like what deal the Lotus struck with the eldritch Man in the Wall.

While Warframe's narrative has flirted with the idea of sending players to Tau for years now, the reality of finally heading to a brand new star chart feels monumental.

This could be Warframe's version of The Final Shape -- and while players may not be putting the beatdown on this game's eldritch god quite yet, this is Digital Extremes' chance to build out an expansion to its sci-fi universe that is completely divorced from the imagery and cultural norms associated with our real world. Warframe is already wild, but the Tau system presents an opportunity to explore ideas unbound by the established conventions of the Origin System.

That's not to say everything will be set in stone when Tau comes to the live build. The new star system will be slowly built out after this initial narrative adventure, which features a distinctly detective-noir vibe, naturally paired with a fedora-wearing, smooth-talking Warframe named Brysko.

items.title: TennoCon 2026 Relay Experience
items.summary: This year's TennoCon Relay Experience is now available to join! Jump in-game and head to the TennoLive 2026 Relay orbiting Saturn. Once you arrive, head straight to the Dry Dock and get ready for the show to begin at 4:30 p.m. ET. Please no
items.content: This year's TennoCon Relay Experience is now available to join! Jump in-game and head to the TennoLive 2026 Relay orbiting Saturn. Once you arrive, head straight to the Dry Dock and get ready for the show to begin at 4:30 p.m. ET. Please note: in order to access the Relay you must have completed at least one Mission on the Star Chart. See you there, Tenno!

items.title: Mesa Heirloom Collection Available Now
items.summary: The latest to join the hallowed halls of Heirlooms has launched! The legendary gunslinger receives a bounty of new Customizations through the new Mesa Heirloom Collection, featuring her signature brimmed Signa and travel-worn Duster attachm
items.content:
The latest to join the hallowed halls of Heirlooms has launched! The legendary gunslinger receives a bounty of new Customizations through the new Mesa Heirloom Collection, featuring her signature brimmed Signa and travel-worn Duster attachments to further adjust the loadout to your liking. Heirloom Skins signify the passage of time and the dedication of the Tenno, honoring the community and legacy of Warframes. You can choose to either purchase the Collection via the in-game Market for Platinum, or choose to pay in your local currency (and receive an additional 425 Platinum). https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735410/Warframe_Mesa_Heirloom_Collection/

The Mesa Heirloom Collection includes:

  • Mesa Heirloom Skina
  • Mesa Heirloom Signa
  • Mesa Heirloom Color Palette
  • Mesa Heirloom Glyph
  • Mesa Heirloom Sigil
  • Mesa Heirloom Prex Card
--

Don't delay, partner. Get your Mesa Heirloom Collection today!

items.title: TennoCon 2026 Merch Available Now!
items.summary: This year’s TennoCon collection has arrived! From cozy Floofs and premium apparel to collectible pins and brand-new collaborations, there’s something for every Tenno in the official Merch Store . New Favorites Amir and Lettie Floofs Bring t
items.content:
This year’s TennoCon collection has arrived! From cozy Floofs and premium apparel to collectible pins and brand-new collaborations, there’s something for every Tenno in the official Merch Store.

New Favorites

Amir and Lettie Floofs Bring two members of the Hex home in irresistibly huggable Floof form. Whether they’re guarding your Orbiter or brightening your desk, Amir and Lettie are ready for their next mission. Lotus Spirit Jersey Created in collaboration with Spirit Jersey, this premium heavyweight jersey lets you carry the Lotus’s guidance wherever you go. Champion Stalker Jersey Made in collaboration with Champion, honor the Origin System’s most relentless hunter with this bold Stalker Jersey. THE LINE x WARFRAME: 1999 T-Shirt Inspired by the world of Warframe: 1999, this collaboration with THE LINE brings Höllvania’s unmistakable style to your everyday Arsenal.

Collectibles and Collaborations

Sling Bag 2.0 Carry your everyday essentials in Warframe style with the latest evolutions of our popular sling bag. Liset Pool Float Take your trusty Landing Craft on your next summer adventure with the Liset Pool Float. *Note: This item will only be available in select countries. See store.warframe.com for more details. Pin-Maggedon Expand your collection with 16 brand-new collectible pins, featuring fan-favorite Warframes, characters, creatures and more. Plus More! Also available are the Red Lotus Logo Hoodie, TennoConcert 2026 T-Shirt, Vinyl Slipmat, magnets and more. --

Buy Now

Quantities are limited, so don’t miss your chance to add these exclusive items to your Arsenal!

Darvo Deals at TennoCon 2026

Where there’s an opportunity to turn a profit, Darvo shall appear — and he’s slashing prices just in time for TennoCon 2026! From now until July 31, save up to 30% off on select Warframe Merch, including T-shirts, plushies, posters and plenty more!

  • Conquera Virmink
  • Sunny Pobber Floof

  • Ascension Day Wall Scroll
If anything above catches your attention, don’t wait! Darvo’s Merch deals are available in limited quantities until July 31.
